project.name: china-slot-game
project.description: Browser-based Chinese-themed slot machine game built with Phaser, plus server-side implementation examples for RNG, sessions, spin APIs, and database schema.

nodes.name: IMPLEMENTATION_CHECKLIST.md
nodes.summary: Phase-by-phase checklist for server setup, authentication, game logic, APIs, database integration, testing, deployment, and monitoring.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Explains the server example files, setup steps, API test commands, and production hardening work that remains.

nodes.name: SERVER_IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md
nodes.summary: Detailed implementation plan for moving the slot game to server-authoritative RNG, persistence, security, monitoring, and deployment.

nodes.name: database_schema.sql
nodes.summary: PostgreSQL schema for players, sessions, spins, balance transactions, RTP tracking, indexes, and reporting views.

nodes.name: package.json
nodes.summary: Declares the example server runtime, development scripts, and dependencies for Express, JWT, PostgreSQL, security, and testing.

nodes.name: OFL.txt
nodes.summary: Contains the SIL Open Font License governing the bundled Vollkorn font assets.

nodes.name: README.txt
nodes.summary: Documents the bundled Vollkorn font family, font files, and source metadata used by the bitmap font assets.

nodes.name: index.html
nodes.summary: Bootstraps the browser game, centers the canvas, loads Phaser, and includes the slot game scripts in dependency order.

edges.description: SlotGame reads the slotConfig3x5 object for rules, assets, controls, and reels.
SlotGame instantiates reels, controls, player, win, and payline classes.
SlotGame creates state objects and changes states after spin outcomes.
SlotGame uses shared event, audio, popup, and coroutine utilities.
Popup handlers use popup controller utilities from mkutils.
Slot domain classes use shared event and tween helper abstractions.
State classes drive slot controls, reels, and win handling objects.
Client integration example targets the Express API shape in server.js.
The example server is intended to replace memory maps with the provided PostgreSQL schema.
package.json defines scripts and dependencies for the example Express server.
Server documentation describes the server implementation path.
Server documentation describes client/server integration work.
Server documentation describes the server implementation path.
Server documentation describes client/server integration work.
Server documentation describes the server implementation path.
Server documentation describes client/server integration work.
Font README and license document the bundled Vollkorn font assets.

layers.name: Browser Shell
layers.description: HTML entry point and browser script loading surface.

layers.name: Game Runtime
layers.description: Phaser scene, state machine, slot domain model, utilities, and gameplay configuration.

layers.name: Server Examples
layers.description: Example Express API, client integration, server package config, and database schema artifacts.

layers.name: Server Documentation
layers.description: Implementation plan, checklist, and README for server-authoritative slot game work.

layers.name: Asset Licenses
layers.description: Bundled font documentation and licensing materials.

tour.title: Start at the Browser Entry Point
tour.description: Open the page shell first to see how Phaser and the slot scripts are loaded into the browser.

tour.title: Understand Runtime Composition
tour.description: Follow SlotGame to see asset preload, controller setup, state-machine wiring, spin execution, and win/free-spin transitions.

tour.title: Inspect Slot Rules and Models
tour.description: Review configuration and domain classes to understand reels, symbols, paylines, bets, jackpot, player balance, and win handling.

tour.title: Review Shared UI Utilities
tour.description: Look at event, tween, sound, popup, and button helpers that the game code relies on for repeated UI workflows.

tour.title: Map the Server-Authoritative Path
tour.description: Use the server examples and schema to understand the intended move from client-side randomness to API-backed sessions, spins, balances, and audit logging.
